name: Masala Oats Porridge
tagline: Spiced creamy oats with vegetables — soft, soothing, glucose-stable
highlight: Cooked-down porridge style with onion-tomato — for those who need slower-release carbs

hero.alt: Creamy spiced oats porridge with finely chopped vegetables and coriander
hero.caption: masala oats — porridge-style, savoury, soft

healthFacts: Slower-cook style softens oats fully — easier digestion than dry-roasted oats upma.
Beta-glucan 3–4g per cup — exceeds the 3g daily threshold for LDL reduction.
Pairing with vegetables triples fiber and adds vitamin C, A, and K.
No added sugar — flat post-meal glucose response.

steps.title: Sauté the masala
steps.detail: Heat ghee in a small pot. Add ginger and onion, cook 1 minute. Add tomato, turmeric, and pepper. Cook 2 minutes till tomato softens.
steps.tip: Soft tomato = no raw bite in the final porridge.

steps.title: Add water and oats
steps.detail: Pour in 1.25 cups hot water, add carrot and salt. Bring to a boil. Stir in rolled oats and reduce heat.
steps.tip: Use water, not milk — keeps it lower-glycemic and lower-saturated-fat.

steps.title: Simmer to porridge
steps.detail: Cook 5 minutes on low, stirring twice. Add chopped spinach in the last 30 seconds. Off heat, rest 1 minute. Serve hot.
steps.tip: Add a teaspoon of toasted flaxseed for an omega-3 ALA boost.
